segments.text: You think these temple shapes are just random? No, they are not. Here, every angle is designed for a specific purpose. First, in the north, the goal
was to reach the heaven. Then build the Nagara style temple, a curved tower designed to mimic a mountain peak. It focused all the attention on the single
point above the sanctum. But the south needed to mark a massive sacred boundary for their community. So they built a dravida style huge walls and towering
gateways called Gopurams to define a holy city. But this created a new problem. The walls were long and straight and because of that they were running out of space for the sculptures.
So the Hoistla brothers used genius geometry the star shape. They folded the wall like an accordion. The zigzag design maximized the surface area on the
small footprint. Now they have a neverending canvas for the stories without building a mild hum wall. For more interesting design stories, subscribe.
